title: PURR Act of 2025
plain_summary: The PURR Act of 2025 gives the FDA complete control over pet food rules for dogs and cats across the whole country. This means states and cities can no longer make their own different pet food laws, which should make things less confusing for pet food companies. The FDA must review new pet food ingredients within 90 days, and companies can use ingredients that are already considered safe without getting special permission first. Pet food companies can also make certain claims about their products, like saying they are natural or good for pet health, without asking the FDA first as long as they meet certain rules.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1. Short Title; Table of Contents
sections.text: This law can be called the Pet Food Uniform Regulatory Reform Act of twenty twenty five or the PURR Act of twenty twenty five. The law has five main sections. Section one gives the title. Section two explains why this law is needed. Section three creates new rules for pet food. Section four explains who will be responsible for enforcing these rules. Section five requires new regulations to be written.

sections.title: Section 2. Findings
sections.text: Congress has found several important facts about pet food in America. The pet food business is growing fast in the United States. Companies are selling more pet food to other countries than ever before. Pet food companies buy about seven billion dollars worth of farm products from American farmers every year. This helps the economy. More Americans own pets now than before. This means people buy more pet food and care more about how pet food is made and what rules control it. In the past, pet food and farm animal feed followed the same rules. But people now think of pets differently than farm animals. Pet owners want special food that keeps their pets healthy for a long life. Having pets helps people feel better and be healthier. Pets can lower blood pressure and stress. They can even help people who feel sad or depressed. Before this law, the rules for pet food were confusing and different in every state. Some rules came from private groups, some from state governments, and some from a federal agency called the Center for Veterinary Medicine. This mixed up system made it hard for companies to know what rules to follow. A single set of federal rules will make things easier and faster for companies that want to create new pet food ingredients. This will lead to better nutrition and new ideas for pet food. Having the same rules everywhere will help companies know what to expect. It will also make pet food safer and more nutritious. A single federal system will make sure all Americans can buy safe, healthy, and affordable pet food. Pet food is made and sold all across the country, so it is part of interstate commerce that the federal government can regulate.

sections.title: Section 3. Pet Food Regulation
sections.text: This section adds new rules to an existing federal food law. It creates a new section about pet food with several parts. First, it defines important terms. A companion animal means a dog or cat that lives with people as pets. Generally recognized as safe or GRAS means an ingredient that experts believe is safe to use in food. Pet food means any food made for dogs and cats to eat, including treats, vitamins, and ingredients used to make pet food. Pet food ingredient means any single ingredient that goes into pet food. Pet food ingredient submission means when a company asks the Food and Drug Administration to approve a new ingredient for pet food. Second, this law prevents states from making their own rules about pet food labels and advertising. Only the federal government can make these rules now. However, states can still check that pet food is safe and investigate problems like food poisoning outbreaks or customer complaints. Third, the law explains which ingredients are automatically considered safe. Ingredients listed in a twenty twenty four book called the AAFCO Official Publication are considered safe to use in pet food, unless the FDA has specifically said they are not safe. Companies do not have to tell the FDA when they use these safe ingredients, but they can choose to report them if they want. Fourth, when companies ask to use new ingredients, the FDA must respond within ninety days. The FDA must either approve the ingredient or explain exactly what is wrong with the request and what the company needs to fix. Fifth, pet food companies can list certain ingredients as sometimes present instead of always present. This applies to fats, oils, flavors, and grains. The label must use words like or, and slash or, or contains one or more of the following to show that the ingredient might not always be there. Sixth, ingredients must be listed in order from most to least by weight. However, if an ingredient makes up two percent or less of the food, it can be listed at the end with a statement like contains two percent or less of this ingredient. Seventh, pet food companies can make certain claims about their products without getting permission first from the FDA, as long as the claims are true and not misleading. These include claims about controlling hairballs, cleaning teeth and freshening breath, and supporting urinary health. Companies can also call their pet food human grade if they follow all the same rules that apply to human food and clearly label the product as pet food. Companies can call their pet food natural if all ingredients come from plants, animals, or minerals and are processed only in natural ways. If the food contains artificial vitamins or minerals, the label must include a disclaimer that lists these additions. The disclaimer must appear next to the word natural and be at least half the size of the word natural. Eighth, the FDA must write guidance documents about several pet food topics within eighteen months. These topics include how to prove pet food is nutritionally complete, how to measure energy in pet food, how to document testing, how to support calorie claims, how to handle normal variations in testing, and how to use a specific vitamin compound safely. The guidance should be based on the twenty twenty four AAFCO Official Publication.

sections.title: Section 4. Responsibilities
sections.text: This section adds new rules about who will handle pet food regulation within the federal government. The Secretary of Health and Human Services must give the job of overseeing pet food to the Director of the Center for Veterinary Medicine. This director will have several specific duties. They must review requests for new pet food ingredients using scientific methods. They must send a yearly report to Congress about how well they are doing their job. This report must cover how quickly they review ingredient requests, how fast they write guidance documents, and how well they create new regulations. The director must also teach pet owners, veterinarians, and pet food companies about pet food safety and nutrition. They must help consumers understand pet food safety. Finally, they must do research to help make better decisions about pet food rules. The terms companion animal, pet food, and pet food ingredient submission have the same meanings as defined in the previous section.

sections.title: Section 5. Regulations
sections.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services, working with the head of the Food and Drug Administration, must write new regulations to carry out this law. They must publish proposed regulations within one year of when this law takes effect. They must finalize these regulations within two years. The first regulations must include specific rules called PF one through PF twelve from a section about model pet food regulations in chapter four of the twenty twenty four AAFCO Official Publication.
